description: ExposoGraph 3.0 causal edge projection — evidence-governed promotion from registry and execution layers
promotion_rule: An edge is PROMOTED to the causal layer iff BOTH conditions hold: (1) Directional semantics: its predicate is one of {ACTIVATES, DETOXIFIES, FORMS_ADDUCT, REPAIRS, INDUCES, INHIBITS, TRANSPORTS} (baseline) OR {bioactivates, detoxifies, competes_at} (Phase 3 execution). (2) Evidence support: baseline edge has non-empty provenance OR a non-null evidence_id; OR Phase 3 edge has confidence in {high, medium} AND non-empty sources. Edges failing the evidence test are NOT promoted — they remain in the registry as promotion_status='registry'.

Oxidized to trichloroacetic acid; urinary biomarker
ADH1B/ADH1C-mediated oxidation of ethanol produces intracellular acetaldehyde; represented explicitly in the source Wave 2 expansion.
CYP2E1-dependent epoxidation to glycidamide, the ultimate mutagen; demonstrated in rodent and human hepatic microsomes (Segerbaeck et al. 1995, PMID:7614537)
Direct alkylation at N7-guanine; principal acrylamide-related adduct in rodent and human tissues (PMID:17872912)
CYP-catalyzed 4-hydroxylation is the obligatory prodrug activation step (IARC Monograph 100A)
Acrolein is the obligate byproduct of aldophosphamide beta-elimination during cyclophosphamide activation; drives hemorrhagic cystitis and contributes to secondary bladder cancer (PMID:10220571)
Intramolecular cyclization displaces chloride to form the cyclic aziridinium ultimate alkylator (PMID:7523912)
Intramolecular cyclization to cyclic episulfonium is the rate-limiting step of alkylation (PMID:8635461)
Non-enzymatic hydrolytic displacement of methanesulfonate leaving groups produces the reactive bifunctional alkylator (PMID:12960109)
Spontaneous pH-dependent decomposition to methyldiazohydroxide and methyldiazonium ion; no enzymatic activation required (PMID:2185966)
Spontaneous pH-dependent hydrolysis of the imidazotetrazine ring to MTIC; no enzymatic requirement (PMID:9327140)
Spontaneous tautomerization to aldophosphamide then beta-elimination to phosphoramide mustard plus acrolein (PMID:10220571)
Bifunctional aziridinium forms G-G interstrand crosslinks (PMID:10220571)
Bifunctional N7-dG interstrand crosslinks drive therapeutic cytotoxicity and therapy-related AML risk (PMID:7523912; IARC 100A)
Bifunctional alkylation produces N7-Gua monoadducts and 5'-d(GNC) interstrand crosslinks (PMID:8635461; IARC 100F)
Bifunctional alkylation yields N7-THPG adducts and DNA-DNA interstrand crosslinks (PMID:12960109; IARC 100A)
MTIC decomposes to methyldiazonium plus 5-aminoimidazole-4-carboxamide at physiological pH (PMID:9327140)
CYP2E1 dehydrogenation of urethane to vinyl carbamate is the first obligatory activation step (PMID:12860588; IARC Monograph 96)
Direct Michael addition yields alpha/gamma-hydroxy-1,N2-propano-dG adducts; dominant adduct class in smokers' lung DNA (PMID:19705912; IARC Monograph 128)
Michael addition/cyclization yields 1,N2-propano-dG exocyclic adduct detected in smokers' oral and lung tissue (Chung et al., Carcinogenesis 1989; IARC Monograph 63)
Furfural is reduced/hydroxylated to 5-hydroxymethylfurfural (HMF), then SULT1A1 sulfates HMF to the DNA-reactive 5-sulfoxymethylfurfural (SMF) (PMID:21538843)
Direct reaction of MDA with dG yields pyrimido[1,2-a]purin-10(3H)-one (M1-dG); quantitative biomarker of oxidative-stress mutagenesis (PMID:10064842)
Michael addition/cyclization yields 1,N2-propano-dG (HNE-dG); selectively enhanced at methylated CpG sites, linking lipid peroxidation to TP53 hotspot spectrum (PMID:1937131; Hu et al., PNAS 2002)
Second CYP2E1 epoxidation generates the ultimate electrophile (PMID:12860588)
Forms 1,N6-etheno-dA adducts mechanistically identical to vinyl-chloride chemistry (PMID:12860588; IARC Monograph 96)
Forms 3,N4-etheno-dC adducts detected in liver and lung of urethane-exposed rodents (PMID:12860588)
Non-ortho dioxin-like PCB; WHO-TEF 0.03 relative to TCDD (van den Berg 2006; IARC Monograph 107, 2016)
Coplanar dioxin-like PCB; WHO-TEF 0.0001; CYP1A1 inducer in hepatocytes (IARC Monograph 107, 2016)
Mono-ortho dioxin-like PCB; WHO-TEF 0.00003; most abundant dioxin-like congener in human serum (IARC Monograph 107, 2016)
Most potent non-TCDD AhR ligand; WHO-TEF 0.3; dominant PCDF in Yusho/Yu-cheng cohorts (IARC Monograph 100F, 2012)
Weak AhR agonist; induces CYP1A1/CYP1A2/CYP1B1 in rodent liver; associated with hepatic porphyria (IARC Monograph 79, 2001)
Pentachlorophenol is a moderate AhR agonist; tetrachloro-p-benzoquinone metabolite contributes to redox cycling (IARC Monograph 117, 2019)
